{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/98574309?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Fushuling/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Fushuling/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Fushuling/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Fushuling/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/Fushuling", "id": 98574309, "login": "Fushuling", "node_id": "U_kgDOBeAf5Q", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Fushuling/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Fushuling/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Fushuling/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Fushuling/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Fushuling/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/Fushuling", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-49820", "cvss": {"score": 4.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:N/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 4.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:N/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-601", "name": "URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')"}], "description": "### Impact
Probo's `saferedirect` package validates redirect URLs used across authentication flows (OIDC, SAML, session transfer, OAuth connectors, and trust-center magic links). The validator only inspected the second character of relative paths, so a URL like `/../\evil.com` passed validation because the second character is `.`. Go's `http.Redirect` normalizes this path to `/\evil.com` before setting the `Location` header. Browsers can interpret the backslash as a host separator and redirect the user to an external domain (`https://evil.com`), bypassing the intended same-origin restriction. This enables open-redirect phishing: an attacker can craft a `continue` parameter (or embed a malicious URL in a session-transfer token) that appears to originate from a trusted Probo domain but redirects victims elsewhere.

### Patches
Fixed in `go.probo.inc/probo` by normalizing relative paths with `path.Clean` before validation, rejecting backslashes (including
percent-encoded `%5c`) anywhere in the path, and re-checking the normalized result for protocol-relative and backslash prefixes.

Self-hosted deployments should upgrade to **probod v0.194.1** or later.

SaaS deployments on getprobo.com are patched.

### Workarounds
No practical workaround for self-hosted installations. Upgrade to the patched release.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-x7qq-m748-8p2c", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-30T18:31:50Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-x7qq-m748-8p2c", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-x7qq-m748-8p2c"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-49820"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-30T18:31:50Z", "references": ["https://github.com/getprobo/probo/security/advisories/GHSA-x7qq-m748-8p2c", "https://github.com/getprobo/probo/blob/main/SECURITY_NOTES.md", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-x7qq-m748-8p2c"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/getprobo/probo/security-advisories/GHSA-x7qq-m748-8p2c", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/getprobo/probo", "summary": "Probo has an open redirect bypass via path normalization", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-30T18:31:51Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-x7qq-m748-8p2c", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "0.204.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "go.probo.inc/probo"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 0.204.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Probo has an open redirect bypass via path normalization
